title: leptosiphon floribundus subsp. floribundus
intro: Leptosiphon floribundus subsp. floribundus, also known as the mountain lupine or purple lupine, is a flowering plant species native to California in the United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory problems, and acting as an expectorant to relieve congestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
